title: Stakeholder Task, Workflow Fit, Authority, Risk, and Evidence · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded agent product and risk charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model.
Compare deterministic code, workflow, model-assisted step, and agent loop and choose the least variable architecture that satisfies the task.
Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Nongoals Escalation
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-nongoals-escalation-dycsor" that uses case 3345 to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Nongoals Escalation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Nongoals Escalation into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Nongoals Escalation, keep Nongoals Escalation connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-nongoals-escalation-dycsor
# Competency: agentpy-nongoals-escalation.
# Case 3345: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def evidence_agentpy_nongoals_escalation_dycsor(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, risk_controls: int = 6, unresolved_high_risks: int = 0, escalation_paths: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dycsor = "dycsor-roscyd-3345"
    assert evidence_variant_dycsor
    evidence_axes_dycsor = (
        "authoritydycsor",
        "interaction3345",
        "toolroscyd",
        "statedycsor3345",
        "evaluation3345dycsor",
        "recoveryroscyd3345",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dycsor)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authority-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if risk_controls < 4 or unresolved_high_risks > 0:
        return False, risk_controls, "risk-uncontrolled"
    if escalation_paths < 2:
        return False, escalation_paths, "escalation-missing"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4 and unresolved_high_risks == 0
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-agent-charter"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Nongoals Escalation structural check is only one gate. bounded agent product and risk charter must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
